name: OutSystems
what_it_does: Enterprise low-code platform with a REST API for application lifecycle management, environment deployment orchestration, user provisioning, and data integration.
use_cases: Automating deployment pipelines across OutSystems environments using the LifeTime API
Provisioning and managing users, teams, and roles programmatically at enterprise scale
Triggering and monitoring application deployments from external CI/CD orchestration agents
Querying application metadata and deployment history for compliance reporting
Integrating OutSystems app data with external systems via REST data APIs in ODC
not_for: Startups or small teams — enterprise pricing and complexity make it impractical outside large organizations
Developers wanting a general-purpose API platform not tied to OutSystems app development
best_when: Best when your organization is already invested in OutSystems and needs to automate app lifecycle operations, user management, or cross-environment deployments programmatically.
avoid_when: Avoid for experimentation or proof-of-concept work — the personal environment is heavily limited and enterprise pricing is required for meaningful API access.

auth.notes: LifeTime API uses service account tokens. ODC uses OAuth2 with client credentials flow. Scoped access per API surface and environment.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Personal environment free but limited to 1 environment with no production deployment or full API access
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise from $1,500/month; pricing negotiated per contract for large deployments
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing in practice. Personal environment is useful for evaluation only. API access is gated by license tier.

performance.rate_limits: LifeTime API has rate limits documented per endpoint; ODC limits vary by contract

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: LifeTime API returns descriptive JSON error responses. ODC REST API follows standard HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deployment requests are not idempotent — submitting twice creates two deployment plans. Read operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: LifeTime API and ODC API are separate systems with different auth and URL structures
Deployment operations are asynchronous; agents must poll deployment plan status to completion
Personal environment restrictions make it difficult to test agent workflows before enterprise purchase
API documentation is split between traditional O11 platform and new ODC cloud platform

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO27001, HIPAA, and GDPR compliant. OAuth2 with service accounts. Enterprise SSO with SAML supported. Regular penetration testing.
